g.title: Exploded View of Solar House Disassembly
g.desc: This diagram shows the wooden panels, hardware, and solar panel separated for disassembly. The components include a large house-shaped wall with window cutouts, a rectangular roof panel, and an arch-shaped frame. A small rectangular solar panel with a textured top and two wires is lifted from the top. Four small brass screws and hexagonal nuts are shown pulled away from their holes. The 5mm thick wooden pieces feature various notches and slots that interlock, while the hardware can be identified by the cross-slot on the screw heads and the six flat sides of the nuts.

g.title: Solar House Kit Parts Inventory
g.desc: A list of 19 items needed for the project. Identify the wooden pieces by their 5mm thickness: one large roof panel, one small roof panel, one front wall, one back wall, and two side walls. Find the electronics: one flat solar panel with a smooth glass-like top, one cylindrical capacitor with two wire legs, and three small resistors with thin wire leads. The hardware includes four small hexagonal metal nuts and four threaded brass screws. Check that you have all these parts after you complete the disassembly.

g.title: Removing Roof Panels and Brass Hardware
g.desc: Locate the two flat plywood panels forming the roof of the structure. They are secured by four brass screws and four hexagonal nuts. To disassemble, identify the screws on top of the roof panels and the corresponding nuts tucked into T-shaped slots in the wall sections just below the roof line. Use a screwdriver to remove the four screws, then pull the four nuts out of their slots. Once the hardware is removed, lift the two large roof panels off the main assembly and place all pieces on the component sheet.

text.tspan: Place each item on its spot

g.title: Detach Solar Panel from Plywood Board
g.desc: Identify the small, rectangular solar panel, which is attached to the center of a larger plywood board. The solar panel has two wires extending from one side that pass through a large oval hole in the board. An arrow indicates you should lift the solar panel straight up, detaching it from a round adhesive spot on the board's surface. Once the panel is free, carefully thread its red and black wires back through the oval hole to completely separate the two pieces.

g.title: Disassembling house walls by tilting notches
g.desc: This diagram shows how to separate the plywood house walls. The walls are 5mm thick laser-cut pieces. Identify the vertical wall sections: one has a large door cutout, another has several window panes, and the side pieces have hook-shaped tabs on their edges. The main walls have narrow rectangular slots. To disassemble, grip a side wall piece and tilt it upward or away from the neighboring wall so the hooked tabs can slide out of the rectangular slots. Yellow dashed arrows show this outward, tilting movement to unlock the pieces.

text.tspan: slide the notches out of slots

g.title: Detaching the outer plywood wall panels
g.desc: This diagram illustrates how to remove two outer wall panels from the house structure. The panels are flat plywood pieces with rounded tabs on their vertical edges that fit into corresponding slots on the main frame. On the left, locate the panel with a large doorway opening and pull its right edge away from the central wall. On the right, find the panel with window cutouts and pull its right edge away from the corner post. Tactilely, you can identify these pieces by the tabs along the edges and the rectangular cutouts for windows and doors. Orange dashed arrows indicate that these panels should be pulled outward to disengage the tabs from their slots.

g.title: Disassembly Complete: Return Parts to Sheet
g.desc: This final instructional frame features the message 'DISASSEMBLY COMPLETE!' in large text. A central instruction box directs you to place all disassembled pieces onto their designated spots on the Component Sheet if you haven't already. These parts include 5mm thick laser-cut plywood pieces of various shapes, small brass #4-40 screws and nuts, and any plastic or electronic components. You can identify each piece by its unique tactile shape and texture and match it to its corresponding outline on the Component Sheet for organized storage.
